evidence_level: B+ weather/success model, C budget-recovery lead until Local Finance 365 join
short_verdict: This is a nature-dependent festival model, not a proven contractor-failure case. The 2020 failure mode was warm winter/ice safety plus COVID-19 and ASF; later success signals point to conditional ice-capacity management, paid core experience, local voucher circulation, price disclosure and global winter-content positioning.
why_it_matters: It shows why demand forecasting for winter festivals must be conditional on ice thickness, water flow, rain/warm-spell probability and alternative capacity.
It provides a success-side control for budget-waste analysis because paid core programs and regional voucher circulation can recover part of public spend and localize consumption.
It warns against blaming an event agency without contract-scope evidence when the strongest public causes are weather, disease risk, safety thresholds and product design.

stakeholders.official_page_or_data_operator: KTO festival information, Hwacheon County notice, festival official site
stakeholders.contractor_causality_status: not proven from current public evidence

causal_chain.evidence: Warm weather and rain weakened the ice-fishing premise; media reported the ice-fishing area was stopped after opening for safety. ASF and COVID-19 simultaneously raised disease-control and demand concerns.
2025 reporting states Hwacheon used long-running freezing know-how, adjusted water volume/flow, made ice over 30cm thick by opening and put rescue divers in daily to check thickness and strength.
2025 reporting shows 1 million cumulative visitors in 14 days, use of local return vouchers, official food-price disclosure and over 60% revisit signal.

2026 evaluation report coverage cites a 722-person survey, average spending of 80,930 KRW, about 101.8 billion KRW direct economic effect, 66.7% merchant sales-increase experience and foreign visitor ice-fishing satisfaction of 89%.

A 2026 fiscal-analysis article reports 2024 execution of 4.655 billion KRW and admission revenue of 2.414 billion KRW, a 51.8% recovery lead. This remains C until the official cost-accounting row is joined.

not_supported_claims: A subcontractor or agency caused the 2020 difficulty.
High public spend itself proves waste.
The 2026 economic-effect headline alone proves net public value without cost and externality join.
Visitor count alone proves satisfaction or local merchant benefit.

planning_model.forecast_unit: conditional capacity, not raw expected visitors
minimum_variables: local voucher redemption and merchant sales
planning_model.decision_rule: If ice capacity falls below the safe operating threshold, visitor demand should be capped or shifted to alternative paid/non-ice programs before marketing volume is increased.
